sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἄζυμος

- Transliteration: Azumos

- Phonetic: ad'-zoo-mos

- Definition:

1. unfermented, free from leaven or yeast

a. of the unleavened loaves used in the paschal feast of the Jews

b. metaph. free from faults or the "leaven of iniquity"

- Origin: from G1 (as a negative particle) and G2219

- TDNT entry: 17:02,3

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From G1 (as a negative particle) and G2219; unleavened that is (figuratively) uncorrupted; (in the neuter plural) specifically (by implication) the Passover week: - unleavened (bread).
